title: 직장·업무 관련 표현
subtitle: 한국어로 직장과 업무에 대해 이야기하는 방법을 배워요

examples.meaning: Which company do you work for?
I work at an IT company.

expression.culturalTip: 한국의 회사 문화는 위계질서와 팀워크를 중시해요.
expression.explanationEn: It means an organization or company where people work.
expression.culturalTipEn: The company culture in Korea places a strong emphasis on hierarchy and teamwork.

expression.explanation: 직업 활동을 하는 것을 의미해요. '근무하다'가 더 공식적이에요.

examples.meaning: I work at a company.
I work from Monday to Friday.

expression.culturalTip: 한국은 주 5일 근무가 일반적이지만, 업무 시간이 긴 편이에요.
expression.explanationEn: It means engaging in professional activities. '근무하다' (geunmuhada) is more formal.
expression.culturalTipEn: In Korea, a five-day workweek is common, but the working hours tend to be quite long.

examples.meaning: Our boss is kind.
I get along well with my colleagues.

expression.culturalTip: 한국 회사에서는 직급과 나이에 따른 호칭이 중요해요!
expression.explanationEn: This is a word that represents relationships in the workplace.
expression.culturalTipEn: In Korean companies, titles based on rank and age are very important!

expression.explanation: 여러 사람이 모여 의논하는 것을 의미해요.

examples.meaning: I have a meeting in the afternoon.
What time does the meeting start?

expression.culturalTip: 한국 회사에서는 회의가 자주 있고, 긴 편이에요.
expression.explanationEn: It means a gathering of several people to discuss something.
expression.culturalTipEn: In Korean companies, meetings are frequent and tend to be lengthy.

examples.meaning: I'm working on a new project now.
I'm busy because I have a lot of work.

expression.explanationEn: It means tasks or assignments that need to be done.
expression.whenToUseEn: "When explaining the details of a task"
expression.culturalTipEn: Korean office workers tend to have a heavy workload.

expression.meaning: To go to work / To leave work
expression.explanation: 회사에 가거나 회사에서 나올 때 사용하는 표현이에요.

examples.meaning: I go to work at 9 AM.
What time do you usually leave work?

expression.culturalTip: 한국에서는 상사보다 먼저 퇴근하기 어려운 문화가 있었지만, 요즘은 바뀌고 있어요.
expression.explanationEn: This is an expression used when going to or coming out of the company.
expression.culturalTipEn: In Korea, there used to be a culture where it was difficult to leave work before your boss, but these days, that is changing.

expression.explanation: 정규 근무 시간 이후에도 일하는 것을 의미해요.

examples.meaning: I have to work overtime today.
I worked overtime a lot this week.

expression.culturalTip: 한국에서는 야근이 흔했지만, 최근 워라밸(work-life balance)을 중시하는 문화로 바뀌고 있어요.
expression.explanationEn: It means working after regular working hours.
expression.culturalTipEn: In Korea, working late was common, but recently there has been a cultural shift towards valuing work-life balance.

examples.meaning: I have to write a report.
The report submission deadline is tomorrow.

expression.culturalTip: 한국 회사에서는 문서 작업이 많은 편이에요.
expression.explanationEn: This is a document summarizing the details or results of the work.
expression.culturalTipEn: In Korean companies, there tends to be a lot of paperwork.

expression.meaning: Thank you for your hard work
expression.explanation: 일을 마친 사람에게 감사와 격려를 표현하는 말이에요.

examples.meaning: Thank you for your hard work today!
Thank you for your work. I'll leave first.

expression.culturalTip: '수고하셨습니다'는 한국 직장 문화에서 매우 중요한 인사예요!
expression.explanationEn: It's a phrase used to express gratitude and encouragement to someone who has finished their work.
expression.culturalTipEn: "'수고하셨습니다' is a very important greeting in Korean workplace culture!"

examples.meaning: I'm on vacation next week.
I want to use my annual leave.

expression.culturalTip: 한국에서는 연차 사용률이 낮았지만, 최근 개선되고 있어요.
expression.explanationEn: It means a period of rest without working.
expression.culturalTipEn: In Korea, the usage rate of annual leave was low, but it has been improving recently.

questions.question: How do you say 'I work at a company'?
optionsVi: 회사에서 일해요 (làm việc ở công ty)
questions.explanation: '회사에서 일해요'는 'I work at a company'를 의미해요.

questions.question: How do you say 'to work overtime'?
What does '수고하셨습니다'mean?
options: Thank you for your hard work
optionsVi: Cảm ơn bạn đã làm việc vất vả
Chúc bạn có ngày tốt lành
questions.explanation: '수고하셨습니다'는 'Thank you for your hard work'를 의미해요.

subtitleEn: I'm learning how to talk about work and jobs in Korean.
